latin: Me Ismaelem vocate. ante paucos annos, cum nihil pecuniae haberem et animus meus tristis esset, constitui navigare. mare semper melius est quam terra irato homini.

questions.prompt: What does the narrator ask us to call him?
Why did he decide to go to sea?
questions.target: He had no money and was sad
choices: He was a sailor by trade
He had no money and was sad
He was searching for a whale

translation: Call me Ishmael. A few years ago, when I had no money and my spirit was sad, I decided to sail. The sea is always better than land for an angry man.
